event: The Virgin Mary enthroned with angels at the Last Judgement
symbols: enthroned Virgin with halo — the Theotokos glorified as intercessor at the Last Judgement
flanking archangels — the heavenly court attending the Mother of God and executing divine justice
arched architectural frame — the vault of heaven, the celestial throne room
golden halos — divine sanctity and participation in heavenly glory
Mary's red-brown maphorion — the traditional garment of the Theotokos, signifying her humanity assumed into heavenly honor
frontal pose — the hieratic presence of the sacred, inviting the viewer's veneration and prayer
description: A medieval fresco set within a curved architectural lunette depicting the Virgin Mary seated frontally on a throne at center. She wears a red-brown maphorion over a dark garment and is encircled by a golden halo. Two large angelic figures flank her on either side, each with wings and halos, bowing slightly toward her. The angel on the left wears lighter robes while the one on the right wears olive-green garments. The figures are painted in the Byzantine-influenced Russian style with stylized proportions, flat gold backgrounds partially worn away, and strong linear contours.
theological_description: In the Orthodox iconographic tradition of the Last Judgement, the Mother of God occupies a position of supreme intercessory honor. She is depicted here enthroned between archangels — likely Michael and Gabriel — as the one who pleads for humanity before the judgement seat of her Son. This scene is part of a larger Last Judgement cycle, where the Virgin's role draws upon the theology of the Theotokos as expressed in Luke 1:48 ('all generations will call me blessed') and her unique position as the one through whom the Judge Himself entered the world. The flanking archangels recall their roles in salvation history: Gabriel as the messenger of the Incarnation (Luke 1:26-38) and Michael as the warrior against evil at the end of days (Revelation 12:7-9). Rublev's depiction embodies the Patristic understanding that Mary's intercession continues eschatologically — at the very moment when all souls stand before Christ, the Mother of God stands as advocate. The Byzantine frontal pose and hieratic stillness convey the timelessness of heavenly realities, where the Judgement is not merely a future event but an ever-present spiritual reality. The worn fresco itself, surviving across centuries, testifies to the enduring faith of those who prayed before this image, entrusting themselves to the Theotokos at the threshold of eternity.

scripture_refs.reason: The Last Judgement scene of which this Virgin enthroned is a part — the great white throne before which all are judged
Mary's enthronement fulfills her prophecy that all generations will call her blessed
The woman clothed with the sun, traditionally identified with Mary, enthroned in heavenly glory
The angel Gabriel's presence recalls his role as the messenger of the Annunciation, the event that made this heavenly enthronement possible
The archangel Michael's presence recalls his eschatological battle against the dragon at the end of days
Christ's parable of the Last Judgement provides the theological framework for the entire cycle of which this image is a part

_meta.title: The Last Judgement: Mother of God with angels
